preview_text:
The model favors Argentina at 76%, assigning Austria only a 4% chance of victory. The underdog's path is narrow; the 72-percentage-point gap reflects a heavily skewed match dynamic in the model's view.

The 20% draw probability suggests the model sees meaningful defensive resilience from Austria, though Argentina's win odds dominate the outcome distribution. A competitive first half is plausible, but the model expects Argentina to pull clear. The spread between win and draw probabilities indicates this is not projected as a close contest overall.

The model and market diverge materially on Austria's prospects. The model assigns 4% to an Austrian win; sportsbooks and Polymarket both place Austria near 12–13%, a gap of roughly 8–9 percentage points. Both external sources align closely with each other on the draw (22–23%) and are more bullish on Argentina's chances than the model (64–66% vs. 76%). The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more accurate; users should weigh the divergence when forming their own view.
